name: ARDELYX, INC.
business: ARDELYX, INC. is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company that develops and markets innovative medicines from the active ingredient tenapanor, a sodium/hydrogen exchanger (NHE3) inhibitor. The company sells IBSRELA for irritable bowel syndrome with constipation in adults and XPHOZAH to reduce serum phosphorus in adults with chronic kidney disease on dialysis.
sellsTo: adult patients with irritable bowel syndrome with constipation
adult chronic kidney disease patients on dialysis with hyperphosphatemia

exposures.sentence: We currently market two therapies from the active ingredient tenapanor, a sodium/hydrogen exchanger (NHE3) inhibitor that was discovered and developed by Ardelyx.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 3 clinical trial ACCEL evaluating tenapanor for CIC
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we initiated ACCEL (ten-03-301), a Phase 3 clinical trial designed to assess the safety and efficacy of tenapanor for the treatment of CIC.

catalysts.event: Expected topline data readout from ACCEL Phase 3 trial
catalysts.sentence: Enrollment in ACCEL is expected throughout 2026, with topline data read out in the second half of 2027.
We are currently conducting activities to support an IND submission to the FDA for RDX10531 in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 13 million Americans suffer from IBS-C
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that IBSRELA can bring meaningful benefit to the approximately 13 million Americans who suffer from the symptoms of IBS-C, many of whom continue to experience symptoms despite intervention with other therapies.

tamClaims.claim: More than 550,000 adult patients with CKD on dialysis in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that there are more than 550,000 adult patients with CKD on dialysis in the U.S. and approximately 80% of those patients are being treated with phosphate lowering therapies.

tamClaims.claim: More than 34 million Americans are estimated to be affected by CIC
tamClaims.sentence: CIC is estimated to affect more than 34 million Americans.
